```
isConnectionError reuses canonical transport regex:

```
- **What was not tested**: The exact live Feishu/DeepSeek ECONNRESET production race is not replayed end-to-end; the strongest proof remains the deterministic local HTTP + on-disk session takeover coverage plus the orchestrator connection-retry regression above.

### Earlier proof (head `bf4c277c9b`)

> Supersedes the v6 proof below, which targeted older head `2fc3ecee2d`.

- **Behavior addressed**: A session takeover during the embedded prompt-lock release window must not silently drop the user's reply, and the injected embedded retry default must refresh with the latest configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` across repeated installs (not freeze the first-captured value).
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `bf4c277c9b`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, freshly installed deps (`pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran the affected suites at this head with `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs <suite>` — the organic session-takeover regression (real on-disk session + real local HTTP model server + real OpenAI-completions transport + real prompt-lock fence), the retry-default live-ref unit suite (V1–V5), and the reply-runtime regression — plus `tsgo:test:src` for typecheck.
- **Evidence after fix**: Copied live terminal output captured at head `bf4c277c9b`:
```
 ✓ surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError  724ms
 ✓ src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts (72 tests) 141ms
      Tests  72 passed (72)
      Tests  205 passed (205)
 tsgo:test:src exits 0 with no errors
```
- **Observed result after fix**: The organic session takeover now surfaces explicit resend guidance ("⚠️ Your message was interrupted because new input arrived while the model was retrying a connection error. Please resend your message.") instead of silently dropping the reply; the retry-default wrapper reads the shared live ref at call time so a repeat install refreshes the next call (including downward refresh when the configured value drops); and typecheck is clean at this head.
- **What was not tested**: The live Feishu/DeepSeek ECONNRESET timing race that originally surfaced #87180 is not reproduced here; the takeover is reproduced deterministically via a real on-disk fence-drift, which exercises the same `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` path. The CI matrix on this head was still running at proof time.


### Current-head proof (`bf4c277c9b`)

- **Behavior addressed**: A session takeover during the embedded prompt-lock release window must not silently drop the user's reply, and the injected embedded retry default must refresh with the latest configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` across repeated installs (not freeze the first-captured value).
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `bf4c277c9b`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, freshly installed deps (`pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`).
- **Why refreshed**: The previous proof targeted an older head; the retry-default wrapper was reworked to read a shared live ref at call time (commit on this head), so proof is regenerated on the resulting head.

### 1. Organic session-takeover regression (real on-disk session + real local HTTP model server + real OpenAI-completions transport + real prompt-lock fence)
```
     ✓ surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError  724ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
```
The takeover error is thrown organically by the real prompt-lock fence (a concurrent steering write rewrites the same session file mid-call), and `runAgentTurnWithFallback` surfaces resend guidance instead of dropping the reply.

### 2. Retry-default live-ref unit proof (the wrapper fix, V1–V5)
```
 ✓  agents  src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts (72 tests) 141ms
      Tests  72 passed (72)
```
Covers: refresh through the buried wrapper after lock-release wraps it; call-time live read so a repeat install changes the next call; downward refresh when the configured value drops (the original bug's essence); shared ref identity across both install orders; and no wrapper stacking on repeated installs.

### 3. Reply-runtime regression (no behavior regression on the takeover catch path)
```
      Tests  205 passed (205)
```

### Typecheck
`tsgo:test:src` exits 0 with no errors at this head.

### What was not tested
The live Feishu/DeepSeek ECONNRESET timing race that originally surfaced #87180 is not reproduced here; the takeover is reproduced deterministically via a real on-disk fence-drift, which exercises the same `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` path. The CI matrix on this head was still running at proof time.


<details>
<summary>Earlier proof (v6, head 2fc3ecee2d) — historical</summary>


- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Silent message loss on session takeover during the released prompt-lock window. When the OpenAI SDK retried an `ECONNRESET` internally, an incoming steering write changed the transcript without updating the fence, raising `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`, which `model-fallback.ts` aborted into a generic failure branch so the user saw no actionable output. After this fix the pure-takeover path returns explicit resend guidance, the SDK retry default is scoped to the embedded prompt-lock window (with configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` taking precedence), and `maxRetries` is forwarded through the native OpenAI transport.
- **Real environment tested**: Clean Docker container `node:24-bookworm` (Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64) running the patched checkout at the current head commit `2fc3ecee2d`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Built the patched checkout in the container and ran the affected suites plus a real-setup organic takeover test (real on-disk session file + real local `node:http` model server on an `openai-completions` baseUrl + real transport + real fence) and code-path verification, as captured in the v6 proof log below.
- **Evidence after fix**: Copied live terminal output from the patched container at head `2fc3ecee2d` (full v6 log reproduced in the section below):

```
$ docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/app" -w /app node:24-bookworm bash /proof.sh
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v6, current head) ===
Environment: Docker container, v24.16.0, aarch64, "Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
Commit: 2fc3ecee2d
--- A: Real-setup organic session-takeover proof (resend guidance) ---
 ✓ surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError 1456ms
AFTER-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
⚠️ Your message was interrupted because new input arrived while the model was retrying a connection error. Please resend your message.
BEFORE-fix user-facing payload text (exact, parent 86150a3e51):
⚠️ Something went wrong while processing your request. Please try again, or use /new to start a fresh session.
=== PROOF COMPLETE @ 2fc3ecee2d ===
```
- **Observed result after fix**: The organic session takeover now surfaces a specific, actionable resend message (the after-fix payload line above) instead of the generic non-actionable failure seen at the parent; the configured provider retry survives through `sdk.ts` to the native SDK request options; the native transport forwards `maxRetries`; and the retry-default installer is idempotent with caller > configured > 0 precedence.
- **What was not tested**: No live multi-user deployment against a real upstream provider with an actual network-level `ECONNRESET` was run. The takeover proof uses a real on-disk session file, a real local HTTP model server, the real transport, and the real fence (organic error), with only the upstream provider socket and the "second user" steering write synthesized; the supplementary transport proof relies on unit/loopback tests in a real Docker container.

### Current-head proof (commit `2fc3ecee2d`, Docker `node:24-bookworm`)

Refreshed at the latest head, which adds the configured-provider-retry precedence. Run in a clean Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`, Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64):

```
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v6, current head) ===

Commit: 2fc3ecee2d

   ✓ surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError 1456ms

      Tests  1 passed (1)

--- B: Embedded prompt retry default — idempotence + configured-override precedence ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  51 passed (51)

--- C: Configured provider retry survives through sdk.ts to native SDK options ---

--- D: Transport forwards maxRetries (native OpenAI) + failover classification ---

 Test Files  1 passed (1)    Tests  241 passed (241)

--- E: Code-path verification at current head ---
installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault accepts configured default:
1088:  options?: { maxRetries?: number },
1098:  const defaultMaxRetries = options?.maxRetries ?? 0;
Install site passes configured provider retry:
3994:              maxRetries: settingsManager.getProviderRetrySettings().maxRetries,

=== PROOF COMPLETE @ 2fc3ecee2d ===
```

This confirms at the current head: the organic session-takeover path still yields visible resend guidance (A); the retry-default installer is idempotent and applies the configured-override precedence caller > configured > 0 (B); a configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` survives through `sdk.ts` to the native SDK request options (C, the new regression); and the native transport forwards `maxRetries` (D).

### Real session-takeover path (organic error, real transport + real fence)

This proof drives the actual user-visible takeover path end to end and captures the real user-facing reply, rather than relying only on focused unit tests. Committed as `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts`.

**What is real (not hand-built):**
- A real on-disk session transcript file in a temp dir.
- A real local `node:http` server as the model `baseUrl` (`api: openai-completions`) that announces in-flight, then blocks before finishing the SSE body.
- The real `openai-completions` transport doing real HTTP I/O.
- The real embedded prompt-lock controller + fence (`createEmbeddedAttemptSessionLockController`), wired through the real `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` / `installPromptSubmissionLockRelease` helpers — the exact helpers `attempt.ts` uses in production.
- The `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` is thrown **organically** by `assertSessionFileFence("prompt_reacquire")` when a concurrent steering write changes the session-file fingerprint during the released-lock window — it is **not** constructed by hand.
- The real `isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError` classification and the real `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch branch producing the user-facing payload.

**Mechanism (deterministic Promise handshakes, no sleeps):**
1. A real temp session file is created with one session line.
2. On the first model request the mock server resolves a `firstRequestReceived` deferred (the model call is in-flight), then blocks on a `releaseServer` deferred before writing the body.
3. `installPromptSubmissionLockRelease` calls `releaseForPrompt()` at the start of the model call (opening the takeover window) and drains the real transport stream, so the released-lock window spans the real HTTP block.
4. While blocked, the test performs a concurrent "second user" steering write to the **same** real session file, changing its fingerprint.
5. `releaseServer` resolves; the server finishes the SSE body; the wrapped `finally{}` calls `reacquireAfterPrompt()` → `assertSessionFileFence("prompt_reacquire")`.
6. The fingerprint mismatch trips the fence → `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` thrown organically → real classification → real `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch.

**Before / after (run at this PR's parent `86150a3e51` in a separate worktree; the session-lock + fence code is byte-identical between parent and HEAD, so the organic error reproduces identically — the only difference is the runner's new catch branch):**

```
AFTER fix (HEAD) — vitest verbose
 RUN  v4.1.7
 ✓ |auto-reply| agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts > surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError 487ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
AFTER-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
⚠️ Your message was interrupted because new input arrived while the model was retrying a connection error. Please resend your message.

BEFORE fix (parent 86150a3e51, separate worktree) — vitest verbose
 RUN  v4.1.7
 ✓ |auto-reply| agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts > BEFORE FIX: organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError yields no resend guidance 483ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
BEFORE-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
⚠️ Something went wrong while processing your request. Please try again, or use /new to start a fresh session.
```

**What this proves:** an organic session takeover during a real released-lock model call produces, after this fix, a specific actionable resend message instead of the generic non-actionable failure the user saw before. Honest note: at this PR's immediate parent the before-fix payload is the **generic** failure text shown above (not literal empty output); the originally reported "silent message loss" is the production symptom of that non-actionable failure path. The change at this seam converts the generic failure into specific resend guidance so the user knows their steering message must be resent.

### Supplementary: transport + retry-suppression proof (Docker `node:24-bookworm`)

- **Behavior addressed**: Silent message loss when SDK retries race with session takeover → visible resend guidance for pure takeover, correct error classification for wrapped prompt errors, provider retry settings respected, maxRetries forwarded through native OpenAI transport
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`, Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64) running patched checkout at commit `80bbe803e2`
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` only accepts `model` and `signal` — it drops `maxRetries`. Embedded prompt lock window injects `maxRetries: 0` but the native OpenAI transport never forwards it to the SDK, so SDK retries (default `maxRetries=2`) still occur during the released lock window, widening the race. Additionally, `queueMessage` steering writes bypass `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, causing fingerprint mismatch → `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` → generic failure branch → silent message loss.
- **Exact steps and commands run after this patch**:

```
$ docker run --rm -e CI=true -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app node:24-bookworm bash /proof.sh
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v5) ===
Environment: Docker container, v24.16.0, aarch64, "Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
Commit: 80bbe803e2

--- Installing dependencies (pnpm) ---
install exit: 0
Done in 47s using pnpm v11.2.2

--- Test 1: Native OpenAI transport forwards maxRetries ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  241 passed (241)

--- Test 2: Embedded prompt lock defaults + override ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  48 passed (48)

--- Test 3: Takeover error handling + unwrapping (agent-runner-execution) ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  184 passed (184)

--- Test 4: failover-error classification ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  83 passed (83)

--- Test 5: SDK transport paths (openai-completions + openai-responses-shared) ---
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
 Tests  32 passed (32)

--- Test 6: Code path verification ---
buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions accepts+forwards maxRetries:
1843:  maxRetries?: number,
1844:): { signal?: AbortSignal; timeout?: number; maxRetries?: number } | undefined {
1846:  if (timeout === undefined && !signal && maxRetries === undefined) {
1852:    ...(maxRetries !== undefined ? { maxRetries } : {}),
1940:          options?.maxRetries,

Embedded retry default (maxRetries:0 BEFORE options spread):

```

- **Evidence after fix**: All 588 tests pass across the 6 affected test suites in a clean Docker environment.
  - **Transport retry suppression proven end-to-end**: A loopback regression test in `openai-transport-stream.test.ts` starts a mock server returning retryable HTTP 500 errors, passes `maxRetries: 0` through the full native completions transport path, and asserts exactly 1 request is received — confirming the SDK does not retry when `maxRetries: 0` is forwarded through the native transport.
  - Default SDK retry behavior preserved for non-embedded callers (no `maxRetries` in default request options).
  - Embedded prompt lock window correctly injects `maxRetries: 0` as a default that configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` can override.
  - Native OpenAI transport (`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions`) correctly forwards `maxRetries` to all 3 SDK call sites.
  - Pure takeover errors produce visible resend guidance. Wrapped prompt errors are unwrapped and classified normally.
- **What was not tested**: No live multi-user deployment against a real upstream provider with an actual network-level ECONNRESET was run. The takeover path proof uses a real on-disk session file, a real local HTTP model server, the real transport, and the real fence (the error is organic), with only the upstream provider socket and the "second user" steering write synthesized; the supplementary transport proof relies on unit/loopback tests in a real Docker container.


</details>

## Tests

All affected test suites pass (Docker `node:24-bookworm`, aarch64):

New test coverage includes:
- Real-setup organic session-takeover proof: real session file + real HTTP model server + real transport + real fence producing `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`, asserting the after-fix resend guidance (and its absence at the parent)
- `buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` forwards `maxRetries` when provided, omits when undefined

target.title: fix(ui): keep session switching responsive
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep chat/session switching responsive by coalescing explicit session-switch reloads without caching event-driven `commands.list` results.
- Harden lifecycle recovery so internal webchat late errors can recover persisted successful output without masking external delivery failures.
- Fix Control UI gateway auth so cached device-token paths wait for a signed challenge nonce, including retry after shared-token mismatch.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/commands.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/tool-expansion-state.test.ts ui/src/ui/session-run-state.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts ui/src/ui/gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts`

- `node openclaw.mjs --version`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Control UI session switches avoid redundant reload stalls while preserving fresh event-driven state, and gateway/device-token auth no longer sends cached device tokens without the signed nonce payload.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS using targeted gateway/UI Vitest files, oxlint, autoreview, and the OpenClaw CLI entrypoint.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/commands.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/tool-expansion-state.test.ts ui/src/ui/session-run-state.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts ui/src/ui/gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode commit --commit HEAD`; `node openclaw.mjs --version`.

target.title: feat(agents): auto-trim lean local model tools
target.body:
## Summary

- add `localModelLean: "auto"` so small resolved model context caps trim heavyweight browser, cron, and message tools without forcing lean mode on larger models
- route the effective model context cap through embedded runs, Codex dynamic tools, Codex side-question tools, and `tools.effective` inventory so advertised tools match runtime behavior
- handle provider/model/default/per-agent context cap precedence, including provider `contextWindow`, provider `contextTokens`, `agents.defaults.contextTokens`, and `agents.list[].contextTokens`
- update schema/help/docs and Plugin SDK API baseline for the new config value

Related to https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599, but does not close it. This PR addresses the small/local model tool-surface pressure lane.

## Verification

## Real behavior proof

target.title: feat(whatsapp): agent tools for group create / add participants / invite link
target.body:
## What

Adds three WhatsApp agent tools so an agent can manage groups programmatically, gated behind a new `channels.whatsapp.actions.groups` flag (default `false`):

| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `whatsapp_group_create` | Create a group with a subject + E.164 participants. Returns the new group JID. |
| `whatsapp_group_add_participants` | Add E.164 participants to an existing group JID. |
| `whatsapp_group_invite_link` | Resolve the invite code + `https://chat.whatsapp.com/...` link for a group the bot administers. |

## Why

WhatsApp already supports outbound sends (`openclaw message send`) and the `react` action, but there's no first-class way for an agent to create groups or manage membership. Baileys exposes `groupCreate` / `groupParticipantsUpdate` / `groupInviteCode` on the socket; this surfaces them as proper agent tools.

## How

- **`ActiveWebListener`** gains `createGroup` / `addGroupParticipants` / `getGroupInviteCode`, implemented in `createWebSendApi` over the reconnect-aware socket (same path as `sendMessage`/`sendPoll`). Phone numbers are normalized to JIDs with the existing `toWhatsappJid` helper.
- **`createWhatsAppGroupTools({ cfg })`** registers the tools via the channel's `agentTools` factory — they only appear when the gate is enabled, and each re-checks the gate (via live `getRuntimeConfig()`) and requires an active linked listener at execute time, so a disabled or unlinked install can't invoke them.
- **Security:** `actions.groups` defaults to `false` because these actions can add arbitrary people to chats — opt-in, consistent with the existing `reactions`/`polls` gates.

## Config

```json5
{ channels: { whatsapp: { actions: { groups: true } } } }
```

## Tests / checks

- Unit tests for the listener group methods (`send-api.test.ts`) and the agent-tool wrapper (`agent-tools-groups.test.ts`: gate visibility + enforcement, JID/participant validation, not-linked error).
- `tsgo:core`, `tsgo:extensions`, `tsgo:extensions:test` clean.
- Regenerated `bundled-channel-config-metadata` (`config:channels:gen`); `check:bundled-channel-config-metadata` passes.
- Updated `docs/channels/whatsapp.md`.

target.title: fix(plugins): guard plugin tool descriptor accessors
target.body:
## Summary

- Treat unreadable plugin tool required fields as malformed before wrapping, caching, or exposing the tool.
- Keep valid sibling plugin tools available when one tool has hostile `name`, `execute`, `description`, or descriptor-cache accessors.
- Make effective tool inventory tolerate unreadable optional display metadata and fall back to raw descriptions instead of crashing doctor/inventory paths.

Related hardening context:

- https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88994
- https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/89013
- https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/89016

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: A plugin tool with hostile descriptor accessors could crash plugin tool resolution, descriptor caching, or effective tool inventory after earlier shape checks, causing healthy sibling tools or doctor/inventory output to disappear.

Real environment tested: Local focused regression tests plus AWS Crabbox changed-gate proof.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `OPENCLAW_OXLINT_SKIP_LOCK=1 node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json src/plugins/tools.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory-build.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts`

target.title: fix(web-fetch): extract full HTML body content
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #82685 by treating title-only Readability output as failed body extraction instead of returning only the page title.
- Normalizes title-only comparison through plain-text markdown stripping, so markdown-mode headings like `# Sample Page` also fall through instead of being returned as body content.
- Preserves the configured-provider-before-raw-HTML order for title-only Readability pages, so existing provider routing remains the first recovery path when configured.
- Hardens the runtime body scanner so fake `<body>` tags inside comments, raw-text containers, and inert/nested `<template>` regions do not replace the real visible document body.
- Keeps the change scoped to the `web_fetch` runtime extraction path; shared HTML-to-markdown helpers, provider configuration, network policy, and public API shape are unchanged.

What problem does this PR solve?

`web_fetch` could accept Readability output that only contained the page title as successful body extraction. In default markdown mode, that included heading-only output such as `# Sample Page`, because the title comparison did not strip markdown before deciding whether extraction only returned the title. In that state, users and agents could receive only the title instead of visible page content. The runtime body scanner also needed to preserve the document-body boundary while skipping HTML regions that can legally contain literal fake body markup but are not the visible body.

Why does this matter now?

The linked issue is user-visible: downstream tool output is misleading when `web_fetch` returns title-only text or hidden/inert fake body content instead of the actual page body.

What is the intended outcome?

This is a root-cause extraction-state fix: title-only Readability output no longer completes extraction, and body-boundary scanning now enforces the browser-visible body invariant before raw HTML text is produced. Successful behavior is provider output when a configured provider handles the title-only page, or visible body text from local raw HTML cleanup when no provider output is available.

What is intentionally out of scope?

This PR does not change shared `htmlToMarkdown`, provider configuration, network/auth/security policy, migrations, schema, public API shape, or the provider catalog. It only changes the `web_fetch` runtime extraction decision and focused regression coverage for that path.

What should reviewers focus on?

Please review the source-of-truth boundary between local Readability extraction, configured provider routing, and runtime-local raw HTML body cleanup. The provider compatibility boundary is preserved: title-only pages still try configured provider routing before local raw HTML cleanup, while scanner hardening only affects the local cleanup path when it is reached.

## Linked context

- Closes #82685.
- Updates PR #89028 after review feedback called out missing real-behavior proof, raw-text/template scanner gaps, and provider compatibility risk.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `web_fetch` no longer returns only the page title when Readability emits title-only text; it continues extraction and returns visible body content when no configured provider output is available.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw `web_fetch` tool execution with the real Readability extractor and a deterministic HTML fetch fixture. External network access was not required for this proof.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran a Node proof script that calls the real Readability extractor, then calls `createWebFetchTool().execute` with `{ extractMode: 'markdown' }` against the same title-only fixture with provider fetching disabled.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  ```text
  web_fetch title-only fallback proof
  fixture url: https://fixture.openclaw.test/title-only-template-raw-text
  readability.title: "Sample Page"
  readability.text: "Sample Page"
  readability.titleOnly: true
  web_fetch.status: 200
  web_fetch.contentType: text/html
  web_fetch.extractor: raw-html
  web_fetch.title.hasSamplePage: true
  web_fetch.text.hasBodyMarker: true
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the fixture forced title-only Readability output, and with no configured provider output available, the `web_fetch` result used `raw-html` with the expected body marker present.
- What was not tested: the exact original reporter page, because the issue did not include a concrete URL or full HTML fixture.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the live proof uses a deterministic fixture so the title-only condition and expected body marker are reproducible. The configured-provider order is covered by a focused regression test that asserts provider output wins before local raw HTML cleanup when a provider is configured.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the new focused regressions failed before this fix: the nested-template raw-text case surfaced fake script body text, and the provider-order guard caught the intermediate regression where local raw HTML cleanup ran before the configured provider.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/web-tools.fetch.test.ts -t "falls back to body HTML when markdown readability returns only the page title heading"` — failed before the latest repair with `expected 'readability' to be 'raw-html'`, then passed after markdown-stripping title comparison.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/web-tools.fetch.test.ts -t "tries provider fallback before body HTML when readability returns only the page title"` — passed; configured provider output still wins before local raw HTML cleanup.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/web-tools.fetch.test.ts` — passed, 32 tests.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added markdown-mode title-only coverage where Readability returns `# Sample Page`, proving heading-only output is treated as incomplete extraction and falls through to visible body HTML when no provider output is available.
- Added coverage for fake body tags inside raw-text content nested within inert templates, proving the scanner returns the real body marker and excludes the fake body text.
- Restored title-only/provider coverage so configured provider output is used before local raw HTML cleanup when a provider is configured.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The markdown-heading title-only regression initially returned the `readability` result `# Sample Page` instead of continuing to `raw-html` body content.
- The nested-template raw-text regression initially returned fake script body text instead of the real body marker.
- The provider-order guard caught an intermediate regression where recoverable local body HTML bypassed the configured provider.

If no test was added, why not?

- Not applicable; focused regression coverage was added/updated for the scanner gap and provider compatibility boundary.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. For title-only Readability pages, `web_fetch` now continues extraction instead of stopping at the title. Configured provider order is preserved, and local raw HTML body cleanup is used only when provider output is unavailable.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No expansion of capability. The change stays inside `web_fetch` extraction and does not broaden network access, auth, secrets, or tool execution.

What is the highest-risk area?

Compatibility for title-only pages: `web_fetch` now treats title-only Readability as incomplete and may proceed to configured provider routing or local raw HTML cleanup instead of returning only the title.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The canonical source of truth remains `web_fetch` runtime extraction: Readability first, configured provider routing second, local raw HTML cleanup last for HTML extraction failures.
- The change is limited to title-only Readability output after plain-text markdown normalization; normal successful Readability output is unchanged.
- The focused provider regression asserts that configured provider output still wins before local raw HTML cleanup.
- Raw HTML cleanup stays runtime-local to `web_fetch`; shared conversion helpers and public API shape are unchanged.
- Related open PR scan result: PR #89028 is the active repair path for #82685, and current main did not already contain this title-only/body-boundary fix when this update started.
- Patch-quality note: the added `null`/`undefined` parser results are explicit scanner miss signals for malformed/non-tag input, not symptom masking; they allow scanning to continue until the real body boundary is found or no body is present.
- Size note: the diff is confined to one runtime file and its focused tests; the line count is mostly regression coverage for existing review findings in this PR.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer review after the updated branch is submitted and CI settles.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Maintainer: review the title-only extraction behavior with provider-first compatibility preserved.
- CI: rerun on the updated PR branch after submission.
- External proof: no exact reporter URL/HTML was available, so proof uses representative deterministic HTML that reproduces the title-only/body-cleanup invariant.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- RF-001 fixed: the author-side code/test changes for ClawSweeper's waiting-on-author review have been applied; after submission, ClawSweeper can re-evaluate the status label.
- RF-002/RF-004/RF-008/RF-009 fixed: title-only comparison now strips markdown through plain-text normalization before comparing Readability output with the page title.
- RF-003/RF-007 stale/current-state addressed: the previously reported `checks-node-compact-small-whole-2` failure is no longer visible on the latest collected check rollup, and CI will rerun on the updated PR branch after submission.
- RF-005 addressed as maintainer decision context: provider-first order remains intentional and documented as the compatibility/cost tradeoff reviewers should evaluate.
- RF-006 addressed: the PR body states that the exact reporter URL/HTML was unavailable and the proof uses a deterministic representative fixture.
- RF-010 fixed: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/web-tools.fetch.test.ts` passed locally, 32 tests.
- RF-011 fixed: the provider-before-body fallback order regression passed after the markdown-title repair.
- RF-012 fixed: the new markdown-heading title-only regression failed before the repair with `expected 'readability' to be 'raw-html'` and passed after the repair.

target.title: fix(codex): before_prompt_build receives conversation messages
target.body:
## Summary

- Restore Codex `before_prompt_build` hook input so plugins can observe loaded OpenClaw session messages again.
- Keep Codex app-server model input and `llm_input` diagnostics on the native thread path without mirrored OpenClaw history.
- Copy hook-observation messages per prompt-build pass so hook-side mutation cannot leak into a later prompt rebuild.

Fixes #88353

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Codex `before_prompt_build` no longer receives an empty `messages` array after session history has been loaded; Codex model input and `llm_input.historyMessages` still avoid mirrored OpenClaw history.
- **Real environment tested:** Local PR worktree after syncing `origin/main`, at HEAD `7758087aa855bf1c602484b34848048d625aa32c`, with installed dependencies and local `codex-cli 0.133.0` available as the Codex app-server command. The proof script imported the current worktree code and ran the real Codex app-server turn-construction path through the stdio app-server command.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```text
  source /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-89004-evidence/context.env
  cd "$TASK_WORKTREE"
  PROOF_HEAD="$(git rev-parse HEAD)" node --import tsx "$EVIDENCE_DIR/codex-app-server-hook-proof.mjs" 2>&1 | tee "$EVIDENCE_DIR/codex-app-server-hook-proof-after-sync.log"
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --run --testNamePattern "applies before_prompt_build|keeps thread-start developer instructions stable|routes Codex model input diagnostics"
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.hooks.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts --run --testNamePattern "before_prompt_build|llm_input|context engine|context-engine"
  OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=300000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/boundary-file-read.test.ts src/infra/boundary-path.test.ts src/infra/hardlink-guards.test.ts src/infra/replace-file.test.ts src/infra/resolve-system-bin.test.ts src/infra/safe-package-install.test.ts src/infra/stable-node-path.test.ts src/infra/watch-node.test.ts --run
  pnpm exec oxfmt --threads=1 --check extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts
  git diff --check
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:** The real app-server hook proof wrote `codex-app-server-hook-proof-output.json` and `codex-app-server-hook-proof-after-sync.log`. Terminal output excerpt:
  ```json
  {
    "hookEvents": [
      {
        "messagesLength": 1,
        "roles": ["assistant"],
        "text": "previous transcript marker CODEX-HOOK-PROOF-B2FAEC50"
      }
    ],
    "llmInputEvents": [
      {
        "promptIncludesHookContext": true,
        "historyMessagesLength": 0,
        "historyContainsPreviousTurn": false
      }
    ]
  }
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` now supplies a fresh copy of loaded `historyMessages` to `before_prompt_build`, so plugins observe the previous assistant turn. The Codex model-input and `llm_input` paths still read from the empty Codex-native history array. The new regression assertion also verifies a hook-side mutation from the first prompt-build pass is absent from the later prompt rebuild.
- **What was not tested:** The real app-server proof validated the hook/model-input boundary during turn construction, but the downstream Codex turn timed out waiting for completion in this environment after the proof point was captured. A full repository suite and a full successful live Codex assistant answer were not run.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause fix.

## CI failure attribution

- The visible failing current-head CI check was `checks-node-core-runtime-infra-file-safety` on PR head `efd74746717cefb83afa2b01c49f64cde1f92414`; its log showed Vitest produced no output for 900000ms and was terminated with exit code 143, not a failed assertion in this Codex patch.
- After syncing `origin/main`, the matching local infra file-safety target passed on HEAD `7758087aa855bf1c602484b34848048d625aa32c`: `Test Files 4 passed (4)`, `Tests 39 passed (39)`.

## Review findings addressed

- RF-001: The real proof still validates the changed hook/model-input boundary rather than a full downstream assistant answer. The downstream Codex turn timed out after the proof point was captured in this environment, so this remains disclosed under “What was not tested”; the changed behavior is covered by real app-server proof plus focused and adjacent regression tests.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the touched contract because the production change remains narrowly scoped to the Codex app-server hook/model-input boundary, and the new assertion covers the reviewer-suggested mutation isolation case.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** History-sensitive plugins using `before_prompt_build.messages` can again distinguish a resumed Codex session from a brand-new session, without re-injecting mirrored OpenClaw transcript history into the native Codex model input.
- **What did NOT change:** This does not change Codex `turn/start` model input history, `llm_input.historyMessages`, provider routing, plugin APIs, generated protocol/schema files, dependencies, workflows, lockfiles, or any non-Codex extension behavior.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** Source-of-truth stays split by owner: loaded OpenClaw `historyMessages` are only the hook-observation input, while Codex native thread continuity remains owned by the app-server thread and current `turn/start.input`; the proof and tests verify that the model-consumed history path stays empty.
- **Patch quality notes:** The patch avoids fallback behavior and keeps the two channels explicit: hook observation gets copied loaded session messages, while Codex model input and diagnostics keep the empty native-thread history. No dependencies, generated files, workflows, lockfiles, or cross-extension public APIs were changed.
- **Target test file:** `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- **Scenario locked in:** A session with a previous assistant message calls `before_prompt_build`; the hook sees that previous message, while the Codex turn input still contains only the current prompt/context and diagnostics keep history empty.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The focused regression locks the broken public hook surface and the adjacent mutation-isolation case without asserting unrelated Codex runtime implementation details or broad session replay behavior.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** availability, session-state, plugin-contract.
- **Risk explanation:** The touched surface is in the Codex app-server prompt-build path, so a mistake could affect Codex turn construction or plugin prompt shaping for resumed sessions.
- **Why acceptable:** The production diff only changes the input passed to `before_prompt_build`; the Codex model-input history array remains empty, `llm_input.historyMessages` remains empty in proof, focused/adjacent tests passed, and the matching local infra file-safety shard passed after syncing main.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The Codex app-server path reused the empty `codexModelInputHistoryMessages` array for `before_prompt_build`. That array exists to keep mirrored OpenClaw transcript history out of Codex native model input, but the hook contract is separate and should observe the already-loaded OpenClaw session messages.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The patch fixes the source contract invariant instead of masking the symptom: hook observation and Codex model input are separate channels, so `before_prompt_build` receives loaded session messages while Codex model input and `llm_input` diagnostics continue to use the empty Codex-native history array. The hook input is now copied per prompt-build pass so plugin mutation cannot alter later prompt construction.

## Verification

- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --run --testNamePattern "applies before_prompt_build|keeps thread-start developer instructions stable|routes Codex model input diagnostics"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.hooks.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts --run --testNamePattern "before_prompt_build|llm_input|context engine|context-engine"`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --threads=1 --check extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: fix(browser): read Windows Chrome version from build dir in doctor
target.body:
## Summary

- `openclaw doctor` on Windows reports `Could not determine the installed Chrome version. Chrome MCP requires Google Chrome 144+ on this host.` even when a supported Chrome (e.g. 148.0.7778.179) is installed at the standard path and the browser tool itself connects and works fine. See #87312.
- Root cause: `readBrowserVersion()` resolves the version by running `chrome.exe --version`. On macOS/Linux that prints the version to stdout, but on Windows the GUI binary does not attach to the parent console, so `--version` produces no stdout and detection returns `null`.
- Fix: add a `win32` branch to `readBrowserVersion()` that reads the version from the Chromium install layout (the newest sibling `Application\<version>` build directory), falling back to the executable's file metadata via PowerShell `ProductVersion` for non-standard/portable layouts. This mirrors the existing `darwin` branch, which already reads the app bundle `Info.plist` instead of `--version`.
- Out of scope: the minimum-version policy and the macOS/Linux code paths are unchanged.
- Success: on Windows, doctor detects Chrome 144+ and stops emitting the false warning; other platforms behave exactly as before.
- Reviewer focus: the new `readWindowsBrowserVersion()` helper and the `win32` branch in `readBrowserVersion()` in `extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome.executables.ts`.

## Linked context

Closes #87312

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No; community bug report.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: doctor false positive "Could not determine the installed Chrome version" on Windows with a working Chrome install.
- Real environment tested: macOS (development host). The Windows-specific path was exercised by unit tests, **not** on a live Windows host (I do not have a Windows machine available).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm test extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome.version.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test extensions/browser/src/doctor-browser.test.ts`
  - `pnpm tsgo`, `oxlint`, `oxfmt --check` on the touched files
- Evidence after fix:
  - New `on Windows` unit tests in `chrome.version.test.ts` stub `process.platform = "win32"` and build a real temporary directory mirroring the `Application\<version>` install layout. They assert that `readBrowserVersion()`:
    - derives the newest version from sibling build directories using numeric (not lexical) ordering, and does **not** spawn PowerShell when a version directory exists;
    - falls back to `powershell.exe ... (Get-Item -LiteralPath '<exe>').VersionInfo.ProductVersion` when no version directory exists;
    - returns `null` when neither signal is available.
  - Results: `chrome.version.test.ts` 6 passed; `doctor-browser.test.ts` 11 passed; `tsgo` 0 errors; `oxlint` 0 errors; `oxfmt --check` clean.
- Observed result after fix: on the simulated Windows layout `readBrowserVersion()` returns e.g. `148.0.7778.179`, so `parseBrowserMajorVersion()` yields `148 >= 144` and doctor would not emit the warning.
- What was not tested: a live Windows host with a real Chrome install. The PowerShell command was not executed against a real `chrome.exe`; the fallback is covered via a mocked `execFileSync`.
- Proof limitations: I cannot run Windows locally, so the version-directory layout and the PowerShell `ProductVersion` fallback are validated by unit tests and the well-documented Chromium-on-Windows `--version` behavior rather than a live run. Live confirmation on a Windows host would strengthen this PR; happy to adjust if a maintainer can reproduce.

target.title: fix: route Discord CLI sends through gateway
target.body:
Direct `openclaw message send --channel discord` currently takes the Discord outbound adapter's direct path, which bypasses gateway-scoped message_sending/message_sent hooks. That leaves runtime ownership guards such as Action Gate unable to enforce protected Discord scopes for local CLI sends.

This changes the Discord outbound adapter to advertise `deliveryMode: "gateway"`, so core direct CLI/core sends route through the gateway delivery path. The gateway handler still performs the final platform send through the adapter, avoiding a send loop while ensuring gateway host hooks run.

Tests:
- `pnpm --filter extension-discord test -- extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:check extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.ts extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts`
- `pnpm build:strict`
target.author: h-mascot

target.title: fix(auth): tighten billing cooldown defaults to recover from multi-hour lockouts (#70903)
target.body:
## Summary

- Multi-hour persisted `disabledUntil` (old 5h base / 24h cap billing defaults) keeps users locked out of a provider for the full window even after billing is fixed. The call layer skips disabled profiles before any probe runs, so `markAuthProfileSuccess` never gets a chance to clear the field. See #70903.
- Lower the billing-disable defaults to ~5 minutes base / ~15 minutes cap so new billing failures recover quickly. Operators who want a longer pause can still set `auth.cooldowns.billingBackoffHours` / `auth.cooldowns.billingMaxHours`.
- Clamp stale persisted billing `disabledUntil` values inside `resolveAuthProfileOrder`, anchored to the original `lastFailureAt`, so upgraded users are not stuck for hours and repeated reloads do not slide the clamp window forward.
- Align the config contract surfaces: runtime defaults, docs, schema help, and exported `AuthConfig` comments now describe the same 5-minute / 15-minute billing cooldown defaults.

## Linked context

Closes #70903

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Persisted `disabledUntil` in `~/.openclaw/agents/<id>/agent/auth-state.json` can block users from a provider for hours after billing recovery. This patch lowers new billing-disable defaults and retroactively clamps stale on-disk billing disables during auth-profile ordering.

Real environment tested: Local macOS arm64 checkout rebuilt on current `openclaw/openclaw` `main` at `6c7644268f59770361d2760a27cb49823ec76717`. Runtime fixture executed with Node + `tsx`, importing the production `resolveAuthProfileOrder`, `resolveBillingDisableCeilingMs`, and `isProfileInCooldown` functions from this branch. This does not use a live paid-provider billing drain/re-credit path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- Runtime fixture: `NO_COLOR=1 node --import tsx --input-type=module -`, with an inline fixture that reconstructs the same stale persisted store on repeated reloads (`disabledUntil = lastFailureAt + 5h`, `disabledReason = "billing"`, `lastFailureAt` preserved), then calls the production auth-profile ordering path at `lastFailureAt + 10m` and `lastFailureAt + 16m`.
- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/order.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/order.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/order.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/usage-state.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/types.auth.ts`
- `npx oxfmt --check docs/concepts/model-failover.md docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/order.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/order.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/usage-state.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/types.auth.ts`
- `pnpm dlx --config.resolution-mode=highest markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc docs/concepts/model-failover.md docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`
- `git diff --check`

Evidence after fix:

Copied live output from the runtime fixture:

```json
{
  "behavior": "billing disable reload recovery (#70903)",
  "defaultBillingCeilingMs": 900000,
  "stalePersistedDisableMs": 18000000,
  "beforeCapReload": {
    "nowOffsetMs": 600000,
    "order": [
      "fixture-provider:default"
    ],
    "clampedDisabledUntilOffsetMs": 900000,
    "remainingMs": 300000,
    "inCooldown": true
  },
  "afterCapReload": {
    "nowOffsetMs": 960000,
    "order": [
      "fixture-provider:default"
    ],
    "disabledUntil": "CLEARED",
    "disabledReason": "CLEARED",
    "inCooldown": false
  },
  "result": "ALL ASSERTIONS PASSED"
}
```

Supplemental checks:
- Vitest: 3 files passed, 119 tests passed.
- Oxlint: clean on touched TypeScript files.
- Oxfmt: all 10 matched files use the correct format.
- Markdownlint with repo config: 660 docs files linted, 0 errors.
- `git diff --check`: clean.
- New and refreshed coverage exercises `clampStaleBillingDisable`, the default billing-disable ceiling, the full `resolveAuthProfileOrder` read path, repeated fresh-store reloads after the cap, missing-anchor fallback, and sibling non-billing/cooldown/block windows.

Observed result after fix:
- Default billing disable after the first billing failure is ~5 minutes instead of ~5 hours.
- Default repeated billing-disable cap is ~15 minutes instead of 24 hours.
- A stale persisted multi-hour billing disable is clamped to the active cap before expiry cleanup.
- A stale persisted billing disable anchored to an old `lastFailureAt` becomes eligible after the cap instead of sliding to `now + 15m` on every reload.
- Non-billing disables, `cooldownUntil`, and `blockedUntil` are not clamped.

What was not tested: A live paid-provider billing drain/re-credit scenario was not run because it would require intentionally exhausting and restoring real provider credit. The compatibility policy decision around shorter defaults remains maintainer-owned.

target.title: googlechat: deliver media as a text link when attachment upload is unauthorized (app auth)
target.body:
## Summary

When the Google Chat channel is configured with **app authentication** (service account, `chat.bot` scope — the default for most Chat apps), any agent reply that includes media (`MEDIA:<url>`, a markdown image, or the message tool's media param) fails to deliver. The plugin downloads the media and calls the Chat **media upload** API, which returns:

```
403 PERMISSION_DENIED — "Request had insufficient authentication scopes."
```

This is **not a misconfiguration**: per Google's docs, [uploading a file attachment](https://developers.google.com/workspace/chat/upload-media-attachments) is **not supported with app authentication** — it requires *user* auth (domain-wide delegation impersonating a user). App-auth Chat apps simply cannot upload attachments.

Today that 403 fails the whole outbound message. This PR makes both media send paths **fall back to delivering the media URL as a text link** when (a) the upload is rejected for auth/scope reasons and (b) the source is a remote `http(s)` URL — so the user still receives the artifact instead of nothing. Upload behavior is unchanged where it works (user auth / DWD).

## Impact (before this change)

- Any agent/skill that returns media under app auth (e.g. a browser-automation skill returning a screenshot or a downloaded PDF) fails to deliver.
- **Sub-agent completion announces** are hit hardest: the announce errors with `Google Chat upload 403`, retries, then `announce give up (retry-limit)` — and the user gets **no response at all**.
- The artifact is often already a public/temporary URL (e.g. a GCS signed URL) that can simply be shared as a link.

## Repro

1. Google Chat channel with app auth (service account, `chat.bot`).
2. Have an agent reply with `MEDIA:https://<some-public-url>` to a DM.
3. Observe `403 insufficient authentication scopes` on the attachment upload and the message failing to deliver.

## Change

- **`api.ts`** — add `isGoogleChatAttachmentUploadUnauthorized(err)`, which recognizes the 403/scope error (`403` / `PERMISSION_DENIED` / `insufficient authentication scopes`). `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` surfaces these via the shared error path as `Google Chat upload 403: <body>`, so the detector matches the real upload failure.
- **`monitor-reply-delivery.ts`** (`deliverGoogleChatReply`) — the normal channel reply delivery path. Wrap the upload; on an unauthorized error with a remote URL, send the caption + URL as text instead of failing.
- **`channel.adapters.ts`** (outbound adapter `sendMedia`) — the path used by **sub-agent completion announce**. Same fallback, returning a normal text send receipt.
- **`channel.runtime.ts`** — export the new helper so both paths can use it.

```ts
try {
  upload = await uploadGoogleChatAttachment({ account, space, ... });
} catch (err) {
  // App auth (chat.bot) cannot upload attachments (403). For remote URLs,
  // deliver as a text link so the message still reaches the user.
  if (isGoogleChatAttachmentUploadUnauthorized(err) && /^https?:\/\//i.test(mediaUrl)) {
    const linkText = [text, mediaUrl].map((p) => p?.trim()).filter(Boolean).join("\n");
    return await sendGoogleChatMessage({ account, space, text: linkText, thread });
  }
  throw err;
}
```

## Behavior preserved

- **User auth / DWD** deployments still upload native attachments — the fallback only triggers on an auth/scope error.
- **Local-file** media is unaffected (the fallback requires a remote `http(s)` URL).
- **Non-auth** upload errors are re-thrown exactly as before.

## Tests

- `channel.test.ts` → *"falls back to a text link when the attachment upload is unauthorized"* (adapter / announce path).
- `monitor.reply-delivery.test.ts` → *"delivers media as a text link when the attachment upload is unauthorized"* (channel reply path).
- Both assert that no attachment is sent and the caption + URL are delivered as text.

Validated against a **production app-auth Chat app**: an agent reply containing media (`MEDIA:<public-url>`) now reaches the user as a clickable link instead of failing with 403 / no delivery.

## Alternatives considered (open to maintainer preference)

- A config flag `channels.googlechat.mediaDelivery: "upload" | "link"` (explicit, skips the upload roundtrip when link delivery is known).
- For images specifically, render a `cardsV2` image widget with `imageUrl` (inline preview, no upload) instead of a bare link — could be a follow-up.



---

## Real behavior proof

Local runs against this branch (post-push head), Node 24 / pnpm 11.2.2, vitest 4.1.7.

### Validation (re-runnable) — all three app-auth upload paths

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/googlechat/src/actions.test.ts \
  extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.reply-delivery.test.ts
#  Test Files 3 passed (3)   Tests 30 passed (30)
```
Key passing tests:
```
✓ monitor.reply-delivery.test.ts > delivers media as a text link when the attachment upload is unauthorized   (normal reply path)
✓ channel.test.ts                > falls back to a text link when the attachment upload is unauthorized          (subagent-announce adapter path)
✓ actions.test.ts                > falls back to a text link when the action media upload is unauthorized         (message-action path — NEW)
✓ actions.test.ts                > does not fall back for a local-file upload-file when the upload is unauthorized (re-throw guard — NEW)
```
Type-check: `pnpm tsgo:extensions` + `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` → exit 0.

### Behavior repro (real predicate + authentic 403)

A repro imports the **real** `isGoogleChatAttachmentUploadUnauthorized` from `api.ts` (not a mock copy) and feeds it the authentic 403 string `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` throws (`Google Chat upload 403: {"error":{"code":403,"message":"Request had insufficient authentication scopes.","status":"PERMISSION_DENIED"}}`), then drives the real `deliverGoogleChatReply` with only the network seam mocked:
```
[with-fix]  outbound text = "here is the screenshot\nhttps://storage.example.com/shot.png"
[control-A] NON-403 (500) upload error -> NO fallback, error logged, no link send
[control-B] 403 but NON-https media URL -> NO link fallback
```
**Baseline** (parent commit, pre-fix) with the identical 403 input: the media reply is silently DROPPED (error → log only). **AFTER**: exactly one text message with the URL inlined, no attachment, status sink fired, no runtime error.

### Scope — #89430 fully covered

All three app-auth `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` paths now fall back to a remote-https text link on a 403: `monitor-reply-delivery.ts`, `channel.adapters.ts`, and now `actions.ts` (message-action). The `upload-file` action with a **local** file path intentionally does **not** fall back (the `/^https?:\/\//` guard re-throws) — there is no public URL to post and that action's explicit contract is to attach a file. It is fallback-on-403, not "always link".

Corroboration: on a live openclaw-server (Google Chat app auth) remote media already delivers as a text link with no 403; this repro additionally proves the **403-triggered** branch and its non-regression controls.

target.title: fix(context-engine): bound deferred turn maintenance with a per-task timeout (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

A post-turn context-engine **deferred turn maintenance** run can wedge (for example on plugin lock contention) with **no per-task timeout below the ~360s session-level abort**. While it is wedged it holds its maintenance lane, so a user message queued behind it on the same session can wait minutes before being processed, even though the model is healthy. `session.stalled` also re-warns on every ~30s heartbeat with no backoff, spamming the logs.

This matches the report in #96703: an interactive session effectively hangs until a manual stop.

## Why This Change Was Made

Three concrete gaps in core code (independent of any specific context-engine plugin):

1. **No per-task timeout was available on the maintenance enqueue.** `scheduleDeferredTurnMaintenance` enqueued the maintenance worker via `enqueueCommandInLane(...)` with no options. The command queue already supports a per-task timeout (`taskTimeoutMs` → rejects with `CommandLaneTaskTimeoutError` and releases the lane), but the maintenance lane never wired it up, so an operator hitting the wedge had no knob below the session-level abort.
2. **A timed-out maintenance worker was not fenced.** Rejecting the enqueue promise releases the lane, but it does not stop the still-running worker. A wedged worker could later call `runtimeContext.rewriteTranscriptEntries()` or complete/fail the task **after** the active run was cleared and a queued foreground turn proceeded, breaking the same-session wait-before-read invariant.
3. **`session.stalled` had no log backoff.** `session.stuck` and `session.long_running` already throttle repeats; `session.stalled` did not.

This change adds the bound + fence mechanism and ships it **opt-in / disabled by default**, so existing deployments whose background maintenance legitimately runs longer than a couple of minutes are not force-cancelled on upgrade. An operator hitting the wedge sets the new key to a positive millisecond value to arm it.

## User Impact

- **No behavior change on upgrade unless configured.** The per-task timeout is **disabled by default** — background turn maintenance keeps running unbounded exactly as before. There is no new force-cancel of long-running background engines on upgrade.
- Operators experiencing the wedge **opt in** by setting `agents.defaults.compaction.turnMaintenanceTaskTimeoutMs` to a positive millisecond value (absent or `0` keeps it disabled). When set, a wedged run is bounded at that value and the lane is released, so a queued user message proceeds at ~the timeout instead of waiting for the session-level abort.
- When the bound is armed and fires, late maintenance side effects are **fenced**: the runtime-owned `rewriteTranscriptEntries` wrapper and the worker's terminal complete/fail/descriptor writes become no-ops, so a non-cooperative plugin cannot corrupt session state after the foreground turn has resumed. When the bound is disabled, the fence is never armed.
- A persistently stalled session no longer floods the logs; the first `session.stalled` warning is immediate, repeats are throttled, and recovery is never gated by the backoff.

### Changes

- `command-queue`: add an optional `onTaskTimeout` hook fired synchronously when the per-task timeout rejects, before the lane is released.
- `context-engine-maintenance`: resolve an **opt-in** per-task timeout from `agents.defaults.compaction.turnMaintenanceTaskTimeoutMs` (absent / `0` / non-positive ⇒ no bound, no fence); when a positive value is set, pass `taskTimeoutMs` + `onTaskTimeout` to the deferred maintenance enqueue and trip a per-run fence on timeout (hook + defensive trip in the timeout catch); thread the fence into the worker and the runtime-context `rewriteTranscriptEntries` wrapper (checked at entry, inside the session-manager rewrite lock, and before the runtime-transcript write) and before the worker's `completeTaskRunByRunId` / `failTaskRunByRunId` / progress writes.
- `context-engine-maintenance`: scope the timeout classification to the resolved maintenance lane — only this deferred lane's `CommandLaneTaskTimeoutError` trips the timeout/fence path; a foreign-lane queue timeout surfacing on the enqueue falls through to the normal schedule-failure path (the lane is computed once and reused for enqueue + classification so they cannot drift).
- `session.stalled`: apply the existing `session.stuck`/`session.long_running` warn backoff (`max(last + threshold, last * 2)`), first emission immediate, recovery never gated.
- Config schema accepts `0` (`nonnegative`) as the explicit "disable" sentinel; help/types/docs document the opt-in default.
- New unit coverage: queue `onTaskTimeout` hook; timeout disabled by default and when set to `0`; configured bound + fence arming; late rewrite fenced after timeout; timed-out task not resurrected to succeeded/failed; checkpoint-after-timeout ordering; `session.stalled` backoff.

## Evidence

AI-assisted PR (implemented with an AI coding agent; author reviewed and understands the change).

**Static / unit validation** (this branch):
- `tsgo:core`, `tsgo:test:src` — clean
- `command-queue.test.ts` — 34 passed; `context-engine-maintenance.test.ts` — 60 passed (incl. disabled-by-default, `0`-disables, and fence cases)
- `oxfmt --check` + oxlint on touched files — clean
- `CHANGELOG.md` not touched (left for maintainers at landing)

**Real runtime reproduction (end-to-end, in a container).** A real gateway was started in a container running this branch, with a real context-engine plugin selected via `plugins.slots.contextEngine` whose `info.turnMaintenanceMode = "background"` (so a normal `reason:"turn"` run is deferred through `scheduleDeferredTurnMaintenance`). Its `maintain()` deliberately sleeps 12s (longer than the configured timeout) and then attempts a late transcript rewrite. The bound was **explicitly opted in** for the test: `agents.defaults.compaction.turnMaintenanceTaskTimeoutMs = 5000`. Two real agent turns were run on one session. Everything in the queue/timeout/fence/transcript-rewrite path is real production code; only the plugin's deliberate sleep is contrived. Logs redacted (provider/model/host/ids replaced with placeholders):

```
[T+0.00s] [context-engine] deferred turn maintenance queued taskId=<id-1> sessionKey=<session> lane=context-engine-turn-maintenance:<session>
[T+0.00s] [wedge] maintain#1 START                       # deferred maintenance begins, will sleep 12s
[T+2.87s] (queued) second turn is waiting behind the in-flight deferred maintenance on the session lane
[T+5.00s] [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=context-engine-turn-maintenance:<session> durationMs=5000 error="CommandLaneTaskTimeoutError: ... task timed out after 5000ms"
[T+5.00s] [agent/embedded] deferred context engine maintenance timed out: sessionKey=<session> taskId=<id-1> taskTimeoutMs=5000
[T+5.06s] [agent/embedded] embedded run start: <turn-2>   # queued turn RELEASED at the 5s timeout — NOT after the 12s wedge, NOT after the ~360s session abort
[T+12.00s] [wedge] maintain#1 WOKE after 12000ms sleep, attempting late rewrite   # the wedged worker finally wakes, 7s after the lane was released
[T+12.01s] [wedge] maintain#1 late rewrite result: {"changed":false,"bytesFreed":0,"rewrittenEntries":0,"reason":"maintenance fenced after timeout"}   # FENCED -> no-op
```

Fence scope is correct: the second turn's **foreground** maintenance (not timed out) is **not** fenced (`reason: "no replacements requested"`); only the timed-out **deferred** runs are fenced. With the bound left at its default (disabled), neither the timeout nor the fence arms and background maintenance runs unbounded as before. A scripted reproduction is available on request.

Closes #96703

commands.author: harjothkhara

target.title: fix: preserve web tools across stripped secrets refresh
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #77826. A stripped runtime config write can take the secrets runtime fast path and produce empty web-tool metadata. Activating that snapshot used to overwrite the still-valid web search/fetch metadata from the previous full resolver pass, so web tools could disappear after a config write even though the real config still owned them.

## Why This Change Was Made

Prepared secrets snapshots now carry explicit provenance for whether web-tool metadata came from the fast path. During activation, a stripped fast-path snapshot may preserve populated active web-tool metadata only when the previous full resolver source config contained a web-tool metadata container. Full resolver snapshots and explicit empty `tools.web` / `plugins.entries` containers remain authoritative clears.

The duplicate preflight checked exact issue-number PR searches and root-cause keywords. I found only closed or superseded work (#77859, #78067, #78268, #82562) and no active PR owning #77826.

## User Impact

Users keep configured web search/fetch tools after config writes that omit unrelated web-tool surfaces. Explicitly clearing web/plugin containers still removes the active metadata, so this does not turn stale provider credentials into a hidden fallback.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/secrets/runtime.fast-path.test.ts src/secrets/runtime-web-tools-state.test.ts src/config/runtime-snapshot.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Structured autoreview was attempted before this draft, but the Codex app rejected the helper because it would export repository diffs to an external review/model service under the current workspace policy. I did not route around that policy.

AI assistance disclosure: this draft PR was prepared with Codex assistance. No redacted agent transcript is included because the transcript workflow requires explicit user approval before adding local session logs to a PR body.


---

**Runtime proof — captured before/after (negative-control harness, Node 22):**

Beyond the colocated unit tests, the fix was exercised through the REAL secrets-runtime state machine — `prepareSecretsRuntimeSnapshot` (resolver + fast path), `activateSecretsRuntimeSnapshot`, and the **real config-write refresh handler** `finalizeRuntimeSnapshotWrite` (`src/config/runtime-snapshot.ts:294`, the exact path the gateway invokes on every config write). This is an internal-state bug, so there is no external boundary to mock — the harness controls only config inputs and reads back `getActiveRuntimeWebToolsMetadata()`. Same harness, run on `main` and on this branch.

_Before — unfixed `main` (exit 1):_

```
Scenario 1  stripped fast-path refresh must PRESERVE active web tools
  active BEFORE refresh: search.selectedProvider="brave" providerSource="configured"
  active AFTER  refresh: search.selectedProvider=undefined providerSource="none"
  [FAIL] stripped refresh did NOT preserve active web_search  (the #77826 bug)
RESULT: FAIL — #77826 bug signature.
```

_After — this branch (exit 0):_

```
Scenario 1 (stripped refresh)                  active AFTER: selectedProvider="brave"     [PASS] preserved
Scenario 2 (canonical web-config deletion, P1) active AFTER: selectedProvider=undefined   [PASS] cleared
Scenario 3 (stripped refresh + plugin entry)   active AFTER: selectedProvider="brave"     [PASS] preserved
RESULT: PASS — stripped refreshes preserve active web tools; canonical deletion still clears.
```

Same harness + inputs: Scenarios 1 & 3 flip from `undefined`/`"none"` (clobbered) on `main` to `"brave"` (preserved) on this branch, while Scenario 2 (the P1 deletion-clears) holds on both. Supporting colocated tests (`runtime-web-tools-state.test.ts`, `runtime.fast-path.test.ts`): 16 passed.

**What was not tested:** no live external web-search/fetch HTTP call (the defect is purely internal snapshot-activation state); the config-write refresh is driven via the real `finalizeRuntimeSnapshotWrite` handler rather than a full live gateway hot-reload (same handler the gateway uses).

commands.author: lin-hongkuan

target.title: fix(zai): send GLM thinking params
target.body:
## Summary
- Update the Z.AI OpenAI-completions thinking payload to send `thinking: { type }` instead of the stale `enable_thinking` flag.
- Forward the selected reasoning effort as `reasoning_effort` for Z.AI reasoning requests.
- Add a regression test covering GLM-5.2/Z.AI request params and refresh the compat comment.

## Issue
Fixes #97772

## Changes
- Z.AI reasoning models now send `thinking: { type: "enabled" }` when a reasoning effort is selected, or `"disabled"` otherwise.
- Z.AI request payloads no longer include `enable_thinking`.
- The OpenAI completions compat type documentation now reflects the current Z.AI format.

## Testing
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts -t 'Z.AI thinking params|forwards simple stop sequences'`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts packages/llm-core/src/types.ts`
- `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`
- Added-line secret scan: PASS


## What Problem This Solves
Issue #97772 reports that Z.AI/GLM-5.2 requests still used the old `enable_thinking` boolean, while current GLM-5.2-compatible Z.AI requests expect a `thinking: { "type": "enabled" | "disabled" }` object. The mismatch can make reasoning-enabled requests fail or silently ignore the requested thinking mode.

## Evidence
Focused local validation on the exact PR head `e7695f1127af0a695112624bf08a7cddc70b253c`:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts -t 'Z.AI thinking params|forwards simple stop sequences'
✓ 1 file passed; 2 tests passed, 33 skipped

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts
✓ 1 file passed; 35 tests passed

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts packages/llm-core/src/types.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

git diff --check && git diff --cached --check
PASS

Added-line secret scan
PASS
```

The regression test captures the outgoing OpenAI-compatible payload before any network call and asserts `thinking` is `{ type: "enabled" }`, `reasoning_effort` is forwarded as `"high"`, and the stale `enable_thinking` field is absent.
